overview: Processes DTD (Document Type Definition) documents containing translatable text entity declarations. Primarily intended for XML-DTD files like Mozilla DTD files used with XUL documents for building user-interface parts. Extracts translatable text from `<!ENTITY>` declarations.

useCodeFinder.help:
Activates pattern-based inline code detection using the rules defined in `simplifierRules`. When active, text spans matching the defined regular expressions are protected as inline codes (placeholders) rather than translatable text.

For example, variable placeholders like `VAR1` in entity values can be marked as codes so translators cannot accidentally modify them.
examples: With rule `\bVAR\d\b`, the text `VAR1` in `<!ENTITY dialog.fileCount "Number of files = VAR1">` becomes an inline code placeholder

moveLeadingAndTrailingCodesToSkeleton.help: Inline codes that appear at the very beginning or end of a segment are moved out of the translatable content and into the non-translatable skeleton. This simplifies the translator's view when codes at boundaries don't need repositioning.

mergeAdjacentCodes.help: When multiple inline codes appear next to each other with no translatable text between them, they are combined into a single placeholder. This reduces clutter in the translation unit.

simplifierRules.help:
Define regular expressions that identify spans of text to treat as inline codes (placeholders). Each matching span is protected from translation.

Rules use the Okapi CodeFinder `#v1` format. Back-slashes in regex patterns must be double-escaped when written in the serialized string format.

Example rule to capture `VARn` placeholders:
```
#v1
count.i=1
rule0=\bVAR\d\b
```
simplifierRules.values: Okapi CodeFinder `#v1` format: a header line `#v1`, a count line `count.i=N`, then `rule0` through `ruleN-1` each containing a Java regular expression.
examples: `\bVAR\d\b` — matches variable placeholders like `VAR1`, `VAR2` at word boundaries
notes: Back-slashes in regex patterns must be escaped again in YAML/string notation (e.g., `\\b` for the regex `\b`).

processingNotes: **Encoding detection**: Uses Unicode BOM if present; otherwise falls back to the default encoding specified in filter options.
**BOM handling for UTF-8 output**: A BOM is written only if the input was UTF-8 *and* had a BOM. Converting from another encoding to UTF-8 produces no BOM.
**Line breaks**: Output preserves the same line-break style (CRLF/LF) as the original input.

examples.title: DTD entity extraction
examples.description: The filter extracts translatable text from `<!ENTITY>` declarations in DTD files. Comments are preserved as non-translatable skeleton.

examples.title: Protecting variable placeholders with inline codes
examples.description: Using code finder rules to protect `VARn` style placeholders from modification during translation.
